mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals a tight labor market, which boosts corporate profit margins and consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in GDP growth (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong demand-side momentum, which drives corporate earnings and equity valuations, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint movement of GDPC1 and HOUST fails to predict SPX returns with a 3-year rolling Rank IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: During supply-side disruptions, rising industrial production (INDPRO) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal heightened corporate costs and reduced real earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of INDPRO and CPIAUCSL fails to predict SPX returns with a 3-year rolling Rank IC > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4% signals a tightening monetary cycle, which increases borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS fails to predict SPX returns with a 3-year rolling Rank IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects strong consumer demand and construction activity, which boosts corporate revenues and equity valuations, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint movement of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict SPX returns with a 3-year rolling Rank IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) compress equity valuations by increasing discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances during periods when FEDFUNDS is rising.
